VAL-CORE-001.title: Compute matrix runs across detected GPU count

steps.action: Run focused pytest for compute GPU-detection fanout with --basetemp=/tmp/benchcore-runbook-pytest
steps.expected: Detection path runs all detected GPUs and records per-GPU CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES isolation

steps.action: Execute fixture-driven collect_compute_matrix(gpu_count=8) through the CLI test surface
steps.expected: Metrics show gpu_count=8, per_gpu entries gpu_0..gpu_7, and dtype records isolated to each GPU index
steps.observed: compute_gpu_count=8; compute_gpu_keys included gpu_0 through gpu_7; sampled isolation metadata showed gpu_0 dtypes pinned to CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 and gpu_7 dtypes pinned to CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=7

steps.expected: Runtime uses detected GPU count and exports CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES per GPU iteration
steps.observed: src/ornn_bench/runbook/compute.py resolves detect_gpu_count(), iterates range(resolved_gpu_count), and sets CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES to str(gpu_index) in both run_mamf_for_dtype() and run_mamf_fixed_shape()

source: src/ornn_bench/runbook/compute.py: collect_compute_matrix() uses detect_gpu_count() when gpu_count is not injected and loops over range(resolved_gpu_count)
src/ornn_bench/runbook/compute.py: run_mamf_for_dtype() and run_mamf_fixed_shape() set env={'CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES': str(gpu_index)}

VAL-CORE-001.reason: Fixture-driven runtime evidence and focused pytest confirm compute fanout follows detected GPU count and records per-GPU isolation metadata for each GPU index.

VAL-CORE-002.title: Interconnect NCCL all-reduce uses full-node GPU count

steps.action: Run focused pytest for 8-GPU NCCL argv handling with --basetemp=/tmp/benchcore-runbook-pytest
steps.expected: all_reduce_perf argv contains -g 8 on an 8-GPU node and detection path records gpu_count=8
Runtime evidence shows full-node argv and recorded gpu counts for all_reduce_1gb

steps.action: Search runtime call path for hardcoded -g 2
steps.observed: Grep over src/ornn_bench/runbook returned no matches for hardcoded '-g 2' patterns

source: src/ornn_bench/runbook/interconnect.py: _build_nccl_command() appends ['-g', str(gpu_count)] to every NCCL test command
No hardcoded '-g 2' occurrences were found in src/ornn_bench/runbook

VAL-CORE-002.reason: Targeted tests, runtime output, and source inspection all show the NCCL runner passes the detected GPU count to -g and does not fall back to a hardcoded value.

VAL-CORE-003.title: Interconnect report captures bus bandwidth summary for required tests

steps.action: Run focused pytest for interconnect bus-bandwidth summary with --basetemp=/tmp/benchcore-runbook-pytest
steps.expected: bus_bandwidth_summary contains every required NCCL test with avg_busbw and max_busbw fields
Runtime metrics include required bus_bandwidth_summary entries and numeric avg/max bus bandwidth fields
steps.observed: required bus_bandwidth_summary keys were all_gather, all_reduce_1gb, all_reduce_sweep, broadcast, reduce_scatter, sendrecv; sampled all_reduce_1gb summary reported avg_busbw=148.32 and max_busbw=148.32

steps.expected: Interconnect collection populates summary entries for each required test
steps.observed: src/ornn_bench/runbook/interconnect.py writes bus_bandwidth_summary[test_name] = {'avg_busbw': ..., 'max_busbw': ...} inside the REQUIRED_NCCL_TESTS loop

source: src/ornn_bench/runbook/interconnect.py: collect_interconnect_matrix() initializes bus_bandwidth_summary and fills avg_busbw/max_busbw for every REQUIRED_NCCL_TESTS item

VAL-CORE-003.reason: Interconnect fixtures, targeted pytest, and runtime output all confirm that the report captures bus-bandwidth summaries for every required NCCL test with avg/max numeric fields.

VAL-CROSS-003.title: Interconnect AR metric used for scoring corresponds to full-node (`-g 8`) semantics

steps.action: Run focused pytest for scoring from documented section metrics with --basetemp=/tmp/benchcore-runbook-pytest
steps.expected: Scoring uses the documented interconnect source metric and produces valid normalized components

steps.action: Execute fixture-driven 8-GPU interconnect collection and derive scores from sections
steps.expected: AR normalization reads the full-node all_reduce_1gb bus-bandwidth metric produced by -g 8 semantics
steps.observed: AR component source resolved to interconnect.bus_bandwidth_summary.all_reduce_1gb.avg_busbw; full-node fixture recorded gpu_count=8, observed_gpu_count=8, argv ending in '-g 8', avg_busbw=148.32, and derived_ar_component=1.0

steps.expected: Scoring documents a stable AR source path and full-node reference value

source: src/ornn_bench/scoring.py: SECTION_METRIC_REFS['ar'] is 148.32, matching the full-node all_reduce_1gb fixture used for normalization

VAL-CROSS-003.reason: The scoring path is explicitly documented, the full-node fixture produces -g 8 semantics and 148.32 bus bandwidth, and deriving scores from that fixture yields a normalized AR component of 1.0.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Validation used fixture-driven pytest and source-backed CLI evidence on the macOS non-GPU host, with no blockers encountered.
